name: Swift Title Loans
description_short: Swift Title Loans offers car title loans from $100–$50,000 with same-day funding in Memphis, TN. Accepts applicants with bad credit and past bankruptcies.
description_long:
Swift Title Loans operates a physical location at 1473 S Trezevant St in Memphis, Tennessee, providing title-secured loans to consumers who need quick access to cash. The company markets itself as welcoming to borrowers with poor credit histories and past bankruptcy filings, positioning itself as an accessible alternative to traditional lenders. The business operates Monday–Friday 9am–7pm and Saturday 10am–5pm, with a phone line and email support. Online application is available with the promise of same-day funding if applicants sign up by noon.

The company's core service is car title loans in the $100–$2,000 and $2,000–$50,000 ranges. Borrowers can access funds while retaining possession of their vehicle, and the title is returned upon full repayment. The application process is described as online-friendly with a three-step workflow: easy online application, receipt of cash while keeping the car, and repayment to retrieve the title. The company emphasizes security with 256-bit SSL and TLS encryption for online transactions.

A key distinguishing feature is the consistent mention of "Alfred" across multiple customer reviews, suggesting a standout staff member who handles applications with patience and clarity. Customer feedback indicates competitive rates compared to regional finance alternatives and a professional, welcoming office environment. The company explicitly targets borrowers with bad credit or no collateral, positioning itself as a solution for those rejected by traditional lenders.

However, important caveats apply. Title loans are high-cost debt products with typically steep interest rates not disclosed on the website. Customers repeatedly suggest operational improvements (better signage, online payment tracking, payment reminders, expanded hours, additional payment plans), indicating gaps in service delivery and customer experience. No APR, terms, or fee structure is disclosed on the website, making it impossible to assess true cost to borrowers. The reviews, while positive about staff, do not address the actual financial burden of the loan product itself.
pros: Same-day funding available if application completed by noon
Accepts applicants with bad credit and past bankruptcies
Loan amounts range from $100–$50,000, flexible for various needs
Borrowers keep their vehicle while holding the loan
Consistent positive feedback about staff member Alfred's patience and clarity
SSL/TLS encryption for secure online transactions
Competitive rates compared to other regional finance options per customer reviews
cons: No APR, interest rates, or fee structure disclosed on website—true cost of borrowing is hidden
Title loans are high-cost debt products that can trap borrowers in cycles of debt
Multiple customer reviews suggest poor operational execution: unclear signage, limited payment options, no text/app payment reminders, limited weekend hours
No transparent information about loan terms, repayment schedules, or consequences of default
Office atmosphere improvements repeatedly requested (better lighting, refreshments, parking expansion) suggest underfunded or declining facility
best_for: Consumers with bad credit or past bankruptcies rejected by traditional lenders seeking quick emergency cash
Vehicle owners needing $100–$50,000 rapidly with same-day or next-day funding
Borrowers who want to retain vehicle access while using it as collateral
diagnosis: Swift Title Loans is best for borrowers with damaged credit who need $100–$50,000 fast and own a vehicle they can use as collateral. The primary caveat is that title loans are predatory debt products with undisclosed but typically very high interest rates (often 100%+ APR), creating serious financial risk despite the welcoming staff and fast funding.
services: Same-day funding (if application completed by noon)
Collateral-based personal loans using vehicle title
Loans for applicants with bad credit
Loans for applicants with past bankruptcies
